title: MACOM to Enter into Agreements to Further Strengthen Supply Chain
author: Na
description: MACOM Technology Solutions announced it will invest £45 million in IQE plc through equity and a convertible loan note, while simultaneously entering into long-term supply agreements for epitaxial services. The investment aims to strengthen MACOM's supply chain resilience and help IQE improve its financial performance. MACOM will receive board representation as part of the deal.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: MACOM is securing long-term supply agreements and strengthening supply chain resilience through a strategic investment, which supports its growth strategy and reduces supply chain risks.

IQE receives a £45 million investment to improve its balance sheet and financial performance, plus gains a strategic partnership with MACOM, a leading semiconductor supplier, which provides financial stability and business support.

title: Forget Nvidia And Broadcom, These 5 Small-Cap Chip Stocks Could Rally Big This Year
author: Piero Cingari
description: Bank of America semiconductor analyst Vivek Arya identifies five small- and mid-cap chip stocks positioned for outperformance in 2026, driven by AI infrastructure spending, rising chip complexity, and aerospace/defense demand. The stocks include Credo Technology (AI connectivity leader), MKS Inc (wafer equipment), Advanced Energy Industries (AI power demand), Teradyne (chip testing), and MACOM (optics and defense exposure).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Fastest-growing name in the group with 75% market share in active electrical cables for AI servers. Market expected to expand to $7B by 2030 from $1.2B currently. Bank of America sees 44% upside to $200 price target with $10+ earnings power potential.

Positioned to benefit from multiyear wafer fabrication equipment upcycle and advanced packaging demand. Raised Q4 guidance and EBITDA expectations. Bank of America increased price target from $215 to $255 with 8% revenue and 15% EPS growth forecast through 2027.

Offers exposure to AI-driven data center power demand and semiconductor equipment spending rebound. Bank of America raised price target from $260 to $300 with 11% revenue and 19% EPS growth forecast through 2027. Management guidance for 25-30% data center growth in 2026.

Well-positioned as testing requirements for AI chips outpace shipment growth. Bank of America expects 20% revenue growth and 39% EPS growth through 2027 with 12% upside to $260 price target. Additional upside from robotics and Amazon warehouse automation.

Offers dual exposure to data center optics and aerospace/defense tailwinds. Data center segment grew 48% in 2025. Bank of America forecasts 15% revenue and 22% EPS growth through 2027 with 15% upside to $260 price target. Defense contracts and Starlink expansion provide additional growth drivers.

Mentioned as an established large-cap chip giant with already high valuations. Article suggests investors seeking incremental upside may find better opportunities in smaller-cap alternatives rather than continuing to chase NVIDIA.

Referenced as a high-flying chip giant with limited incremental upside potential. Article positions it as an alternative to consider versus smaller-cap semiconductor names with higher growth potential.
Mentioned alongside NVIDIA and Broadcom as an established large-cap chip stock where investors may have already captured significant gains and should consider smaller alternatives.

title: Gold, Silver Spike To Record Highs As Fed Independence Falters: What's Moving Markets Monday?
author: Piero Cingari
description: Gold and silver surged to record highs Monday as investors reassessed Federal Reserve independence concerns following a DOJ criminal probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell. The move sparked a rotation from Treasuries into precious metals. Financial stocks underperformed after Trump proposed capping credit card rates at 10%, while energy and tech stocks gained. Major indices showed modest gains ahead of inflation data and earnings season.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock tumbled 6.6% following Trump's proposal to cap credit card interest rates at 10%, posting worst session since April 2025

title: M/A-Com (MTSI) Meets Q3 Earnings Estimates
author: Zacks Equity Research
description: M/A-Com reported Q4 earnings of $0.66 per share, in line with the Zacks Consensus Estimate. Revenues of $190.49 million surpassed the consensus estimate. The company has topped consensus revenue estimates three times over the last four quarters.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: M/A-Com reported earnings and revenue that beat or met expectations, indicating strong financial performance.
Analog Devices is expected to report earnings, but the article does not provide any information about the company's performance.

title: Peeling Back The Layers: Exploring MACOM Technology Solns Through Analyst Insights - Benzinga
author: Benzinga Insights, Benzinga Staff Writer
description: The article explores the recent analyst ratings and price targets for MACOM Technology Solutions (MTSI), a provider of analog, digital and mixed-signal semiconductor solutions. The analysis reveals a positive sentiment among analysts, with an average 12-month price target of $121.67, up from the previous average of $101.67.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights that analysts have a positive outlook on MACOM Technology Solutions, with an average 12-month price target of $121.67, up from the previous average of $101.67. This suggests that analysts believe the company has the potential for growth and improved performance.
